_meta.notes: Source: Municode (city-linked). Density values verified against ordinance text. Districts marked density_confirmed=false have no explicit units/acre cap — density is implied from minimum lot area. CB and WF-2 have no explicit density cap found; flagged for manual verification.

LDR.label: Low Density Residential
LDR.density_basis: Implied from 8,700 sq ft min lot area (43,560 ÷ 8,700 ≈ 5.0 u/a); no explicit density cap stated
LDR.notes: Single-family only by right. ADU as special land use (Sec. 40-525). Planned Residential Development as special use. Roofline: pitched 4:12–12:12 at/above 22 ft. Rear setback subject to EGLE Critical Dune requirements where applicable.

MDR.label: Moderate Density Residential
MDR.density_basis: Implied from 5,900 sq ft min lot area (43,560 ÷ 5,900 ≈ 7.4 u/a, rounded to 7). NOTE: summary table 40-402 shows 5,800 sqft but Sec. 40-404 detailed standards confirm 5,900 sqft — use 5,900 as authoritative. Two-unit and multi-family listed as special use only.
MDR.notes: Two-unit and multiple-family both permitted as special land use (S*) — Planning Commission approval required. Waterfront setback 25 ft. Side yards may be reduced for substandard lots (note d).

MFR.label: Multiple Family Residential
MFR.density_basis: Explicitly stated in ordinance: 'lesser of 12 units per structure or 30 units per acre'
MFR.notes: Multifamily by right. Efficiency units capped at 1 per 6 units. Min 30 ft separation between buildings. Exterior: min 80% brick/stone/wood/fiber cement. Interior access road setbacks apply (front 5 ft, side 8 ft, rear 5–20 ft).

DR.label: Dune Residential
DR.density_basis: Implied from 10,500 sq ft min lot area (≈4.1 u/a); conservative estimate of 3 due to Critical Dune area restrictions and single-family only use
DR.notes: Single-family only. Height measured from median natural grade, not finished floor. Gable ends must face westerly toward Lake Michigan. Rear setback subject to EGLE Critical Dune Area requirements (Sec. 40-422). Sensitive Area Overlay applies.

NS.label: North Shore
NS.density_basis: Implied from 10,500 sq ft min lot area (43,560 ÷ 10,500 ≈ 4.1 u/a); no explicit density cap stated
NS.notes: Single-family only. ADU not listed in land use table. Waterfront setback averaging applies (Sec. 40-306.10). Sensitive Area Overlay may apply.

S.label: Southside
S.density_basis: Implied from 5,900 sq ft min lot area (43,560 ÷ 5,900 ≈ 7.4 u/a); no explicit density cap stated
S.notes: Two-unit as special use; multiple-family not listed. Exterior: min 80% brick/stone/wood/cement board/vinyl with ≤5" reveal. Roofed porches: min 60% transparency.

E.label: Eastown
E.density_basis: Implied from 5,900 sq ft min lot area (43,560 ÷ 5,900 ≈ 7.4 u/a); no explicit density cap stated
E.notes: Two-unit and multiple-family both as special land use. Required front porch 80 sq ft. Roof pitch max 4:12–12:12 at/above 22 ft.

OT.label: Old Town
OT.density_basis: Implied from 5,900 sq ft min lot area (43,560 ÷ 5,900 ≈ 7.4 u/a); no explicit density cap stated. Min 1.5 stories required.
OT.notes: Two-unit and multifamily as special land use. Minimum 1.5 stories. Required front porch 80 sq ft. 10% affordable units required when ≥10 units proposed in new development. Mixed-use floor area: 500 sq ft min. Side setback 20 ft when adjacent to LDR or NMU.

NMU.label: Neighborhood Mixed Use
NMU.density_basis: Explicitly stated: 'lesser of 6 units per structure or 20 units per acre' for multi-unit residential
NMU.notes: Multifamily by right. Min 1.5 stories. 10% affordable units required when ≥10 units proposed. Centertown Overlay parcels: 100% lot coverage permitted. Non-residential: 70% coverage, 0 side setback, build-to zone requirement.

OS.label: Office Service
OS.density_basis: Explicitly stated for multi-family: 'lesser of 6 units per structure or 20 units per acre'; min lot area 9,000 sq ft
OS.notes: Single-family and multifamily both by right. Non-residential: 60% lot coverage, build-to zone applies.

CB.label: Central Business
CB.density_basis: No explicit units/acre cap found in ordinance. Value of 30 u/a is an estimate. Residential above retail by right; standalone multifamily as special use (Sec. 40-527). Manual verification recommended.

setbacks.front: 70% of building frontage within build-to zone

CB.notes: Downtown core. Residential above retail by right; standalone multifamily as special use per Sec. 40-527. Min 2 stories/24 ft, max 4 stories/52 ft (confirmed from Sec. 40-413). First floor must be nonresidential. Min 75%/85% ground floor transparency. Separate residential entry required. No side yard along interior lot lines. Rear: 3 ft abutting commercial, 20 ft abutting residential.

C.label: Commercial
C.density_basis: No explicit units/acre cap stated for residential uses. Value of 10 u/a is an estimate. Multifamily above retail by right.
C.notes: Multifamily above retail/office by right. Standalone residential density cap not explicitly stated. No side yard along interior lot lines.

B.label: Beechtree
B.density_basis: No explicit units/acre cap stated. Value of 8 u/a is an estimate. Multifamily above retail by right.
B.notes: Front setback: 5 ft min / 10 ft max. Rear setback 10 ft if abutting LDR/MDR. 70% of building frontage in build-to zone. Min 1.5 stories. Min roof pitch 8:12 at/above 20 ft. Required front porch 70 sq ft.

WF.label: Waterfront
WF.density_basis: No residential uses listed in WF land use table. Non-residential district.
WF.notes: No residential uses permitted. Max height: lesser of 2 stories or 30 ft on westerly side of Harbor Drive. Setbacks increase 1 ft per 5 ft building length if adjacent to LDR. No building within 100 ft of LDR property line. EGLE Critical Dune requirements apply.

WF2.label: Waterfront 2
WF2.density_basis: No explicit units/acre cap stated. Height capped at 4 stories/52 ft. Value of 12 u/a is an estimate — needs manual verification against Sec. 40-417.
WF2.notes: Mixed-use east-Harbor-Drive district. Residential above retail by right. Min 2 stories / max 4 stories or 52 ft. Stepped upper-story setbacks: +12 ft per story above 26 ft (front), +5 ft per story above 2nd floor (side). Hotel as special use only.

PD.label: Planned Development
PD.density_basis: Guidance standard: 1,000 sq ft land area per multifamily unit (≈43 u/a theoretical); 50% max lot coverage constraint limits practical density to ~20–22 u/a. Case-by-case Planning Commission approval required.
PD.notes: PD is a zone district requiring legislative rezoning. Any use permitted anywhere in the city may be included. No minimum acreage. Must demonstrate 'recognizable and substantial benefit.' Max height 96 ft absolute. Planning Commission may grant departures from dimensional guidance on case-by-case basis.

CC.label: Civic Center
CC.density_basis: No residential uses listed as primary use in CC district.
CC.notes: Government/civic uses. Government buildings fronting Central Park may be up to 100 ft height.

TI.label: Transitional Industrial
TI.density_basis: Multifamily as special land use only; 0 assigned for screening purposes pending SLU approval.
TI.notes: Multifamily as special use only (live/work). Rear setback 25 ft if abutting LDR, MDR, MFR, S, E, or OT.

I.label: Industrial
I.density_basis: No residential uses permitted in I district.
I.notes: No residential uses. Rear setback 25 ft if abutting residential district.

approval_criteria: Recognizable and substantial benefit to users and community
Type and density compatible with public service capacities
Consistent with City Strategic Land Use Master Plan
Under single ownership or unified control
Feasible phasing plan; each phase self-sufficient with required open space and amenities
pd_process.notes: No minimum acreage requirement. Requires legislative rezoning to PD. Planning Commission may authorize departures from dimensional guidance on case-by-case basis. Maximum absolute height: 96 ft.

adu_standards.notes: ADU floor area may not exceed principal dwelling. Owner must occupy either the principal dwelling or the ADU. ADU on alley lot may exceed district lot coverage maximum by up to 10%.

prd_special_use.label: Planned Residential Development (special land use)
prd_special_use.lakes_ponds_included: Yes — when landscaped, maintained, and part of larger open space areas
prd_special_use.notes: PRD is NOT a density bonus — overall density matches base zoning. Value is unit type flexibility (up to 50% MF), lot size flexibility within the development, and clustering capability (individual acres can go up to 12 u/ac while overall stays at base). Wetlands, ROW, and sub-aqueous land excluded from density calcs. Flag parcels ≥5 acres in LDR/MDR/MFR as PRD-eligible.

affordable_housing.notes: Inclusionary requirement (not a bonus). 10% of units must be affordable when ≥10 units proposed in new development. Affordable units must be indistinguishable in design from market-rate units.
